scores.materiality_reasoning: Retainment of earnings is a direct material wealth and property issue.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to own property/wages is a broad economic right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The inability for a slave to own property is a lifelong structural condition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a legal birth-status changeable only through manumission.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes social assumptions about slaves in his own Greco-Roman world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The speaker's suspicion reflects an underlying factual legal reality of slave property.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave does not inherently define the right to small personal earnings (peculium).

scores.materiality_reasoning: Statue size is a ceremonial honor with minor material weight.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specifically tied to the Olympic games and artistic commemoration rules.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The statue's size is a permanent feature of a lifelong commemorative honor.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Athlete status represents a specialized role acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian refers to existing regulations enforced by the Olympic judges of his time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific, verifiable regulation regarding public monuments.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of an athlete does not include specific statue size limits.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Attendance at major civic/social events is a tangible social participation right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific (albeit major) recurring festival.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Attached to the recurring cycle of the Olympic festival.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is an immutable biological and social category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The social exclusion described reflects the contemporary rules of the Olympic games.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Commonly attested factual social exclusion in the Greek world.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman does not inherently mean exclusion from games in all societies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from legal liability for hyperbole (sacrilege) is a concrete legal protection.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad professional license regarding speech and artistic content.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of the professional role and its outputs.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Eulogist is a professional role or social practice requiring expertise.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian is discussing contemporary literary and rhetorical norms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Argued as a norm ('ancient saying') within a rhetorical defense.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of a eulogist does not technically include legal immunity.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal license for artistic output has direct consequences for protection from prosecution.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to a broad class of creative and expressive life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Covers the lifespan of the professional activity and the works produced.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Poet is an occupational role one can enter or leave.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the artistic conventions of the Roman Era described by Lucian.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a tradition to justify a current rhetorical stance.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Creative license is a social right, not a definitional trait of being a poet.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to use certain names is a symbolic/trait-based resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to the naming conventions of a culture.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A person's name is typically a lifelong attribute.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ethnicity (Egyptian) is a birth-fixed lineage category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes naming practices he observes in his contemporary world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a simple, observed factual difference in cultural practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Egyptian does not inherently require having a theophoric name.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ritual prostitution and manual shaving are both high-impact bodily entitlements.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a local recurring religious festival and mourning ritual.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Restricted to the mourning period and the immediate aftermath (shaved head).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Applies strictly to women, an immutable group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes contemporary practices he witnessed or heard of in Syria.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Detailed description of a specific local law/custom as objective fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Female identity does not inherently involve ritual hair loss or prostitution.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Earnings from labor are a tangible material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Very specific to the earnings of this particular ritual event.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to a one-time episode/payment during the festival.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Applies to the female participants of the ritual.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes a contemporary practice in Byblos.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Descriptive account of where the money goes after the ritual.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Earnings retention is not part of the definition of the gender.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to primary food sources (fish) is a foundational material concern.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific dietary restriction within a religious/regional context.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Dietary taboos for religious members are typically lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Syrians as a regional/religious identity are very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes current religious taboos in Roman-era Syria.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports factual dietary conduct in the Holy City.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Syrian does not mean 'person who does not eat fish'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Food consumption restrictions impact human development and choice.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific dietary prohibition focused on a single animal class.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong religious restriction for the group members.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Religious/regional group identity is durable and structural.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian is describing the lived practices of the region.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicit factual report of a religious prohibition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Abstaining from doves is a rule for Syrians, not a definition of them.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Castration is a permanent and extreme physical/bodily modification.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational change to legal personhood, gender status, and biology.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Castration is an irreversible lifelong physical status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: A priesthood/status entered for life with extreme physical commitment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes the contemporary priesthood of the Syrian Goddess.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the actual rites and physical status of the Galli.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Though synonymous with eunuchs, Galli is a priestly role requiring the act.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Compulsory labor/travel (ritual water-bearing) has direct physical and material costs.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific biannual ritual obligation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Recurring annual/biannual obligation for the population.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Syrians/Arabs are stable regional/ethnic identities.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes a contemporary ritual practice in the Near East.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct observation of a public religious obligation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Syrian or Arab is not defined by water-carrying.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Control over marital status/self-determination is a foundational material/legal freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational marital and personhood right within a lineage.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent change in marital status and social position.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage status as a royal wife is birth/marriage fixed and structural.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to a specific Hellenistic historical event (Seleucus/Antiochus).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific historical precedent as clear fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wives are not by definition transferable assets to sons.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to high-level administrative/military roles has huge material and power payoffs.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of elite military and civic roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A career-level commission with long-term status implications.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility is a structural birth-status identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Set in a historical/legendary period of the temple's founding.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported fact within a narrative where status drives the King's action.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nobility does not inherently define one as a project manager.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from project management and sacrifices represents lack of institutional power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational exclusion from religious and political leadership.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong social and gender-based restriction.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Women is an immutable biological group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Narrative of the legendary temple construction.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the necessity of appointing a male manager as a rule/practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman is not at all defined by the inability to lead temple construction.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from social surveillance/jealousy acts as a social protection/exemption.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to sexual/marital jealousy within a single city's context.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Persists as long as the member holds the role of the Galli.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Galli status is a durable, physically-marked role identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian is describing contemporary social norms in the Holy City.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of social attitudes/norms ('hem thenketh this thing right holy').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Immunity from jealousy is not part of the physical definition of a eunuch.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right of unannounced audience grants direct access to the seat of political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific to a single royal court privilege.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Permanent-till-revoked privilege granted to a court favorite.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility/Courtiers are a stable structural status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Narrative of the legendary King and Combabus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a specific legal/courtly grant in the text.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nobles do not automatically have unannounced access to a King's bedroom.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Dress code and labor assignments are ceremonial and status-based role requirements.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers personal appearance and daily labor activities for the group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies for the duration of the member's life/service in the order.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Galli are a stable, physically-altered ritual group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes current Galli habits and their mythical origin.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct factual description of observable dress and work patterns.
scores.tautology_reasoning: In this context, the group is defined by their adoption of these 'female' traits.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to a sanctuary is a social/participatory role with religious consequence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied specifically to one institution's internal spatial rules.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong (or role-long) exclusion for members of the priesthood segment.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The Galli are a stable and structural religious group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes the contemporary rules governing temple access.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of a rule of exclusion from a building.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a Gallus does not by itself include temple exclusion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusive access to sacred spaces is a meaningful social and status role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Very specific spatial exclusion within a single temple hierarchy.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds while the individual possesses high priestly rank.
scores.immutability_reasoning: High-ranking priesthood is a stable professional/religious identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian is describing the contemporary hierarchy of the Syrian temple.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Clear factual report of institutional access protocols.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Priesthood does not automatically grant access to internal chambers; ranks differ.
